name: Ohio Pharmacy Technician Registration
description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Ohio - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

address.description:
State of Ohio Board of Pharmacy
77 South High Street, 17th Floor
Columbus, Ohio 43215-6126

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Ohio pharmacy technician license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Ohio Board of Pharmacy fee schedule lists a $65 registration fee for registered and certified pharmacy technicians. The board's September 2025 fee notice says the same amount applies to initial, renewal, and reinstatement applications.
Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

mainEntity.name: Does Ohio have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Ohio. To work in Ohio, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Ohio?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Ohio Board of Pharmacy says the application review process for registered pharmacy technician applications normally occurs within 10 to 15 business days of submission.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Ohio while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Contact the Ohio Board of Pharmacy to determine whether a temporary or trainee permit is available while your endorsement application is being processed. Requirements vary, and some applicants may need to wait for full approval before beginning work in Ohio.
